intro: The clearest split between Stark and Accessibility Insights is cost: Stark offers a free tier with paid upgrades (pricing not public), while Accessibility Insights is free to use. Stark is design-tool accessibility plugin for Figma, Sketch, and Adobe XD (contrast, vision sim). Accessibility Insights is Microsoft's free automated and guided accessibility checks for web and Windows. Pick Stark when the job is design teams catching a11y issues in figma before engineering and you accept medium lock-in; pick Accessibility Insights when it is developers and qa doing per-page or per-feature accessibility reviews and you accept high lock-in. The data we have shows Stark at mostly transparent pricing and docs with average developer experience, and Accessibility Insights at fully public pricing and documentation with solid developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Stark is a poor fit for teams wanting automated scans of production web apps, and Accessibility Insights is a poor fit for teams needing continuous monitoring across hundreds of pages. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Stark is for design-tool accessibility plugin; Accessibility Insights is for Microsoft's free automated and guided accessibility checks; decide based on budget vs. support.
verdict_a: Choose Stark if a free tier with room to grow up front matters and the work maps to design-tool accessibility plugin for Figma, Sketch, and Adobe XD (contrast, vision sim).
verdict_b: Choose Accessibility Insights if deep integration is an acceptable price for the depth it buys and the work lines up with Microsoft's free automated and guided accessibility checks for web and Windows.

faq.q: Is Stark cheaper than Accessibility Insights?
faq.a: Neither Stark nor Accessibility Insights publishes entry pricing that lets us compare directly — Stark uses a freemium model and Accessibility Insights uses a free model. Get quotes from both before assuming one is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Stark to Accessibility Insights?
faq.a: Moving from Stark to Accessibility Insights is feasible, but Accessibility Insights is rated high lock-in — expect your integrations and workflows to need rework to match its model. Budget for a parallel-run period rather than a hard cutover.

Accessibility Insights scores higher on developer experience in our data (4/5 vs 3/5). The gap usually shows up in setup friction and daily workflow speed. Confirm it on your own stack before treating the rating as final.

faq.q: Is Accessibility Insights a good alternative to Stark?
faq.a: Yes — Accessibility Insights is a reasonable alternative to Stark for design teams catching a11y issues in figma before engineering.. The practical differences are freemium-vs-free billing and medium-vs-high lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Accessibility Insights as a credible swap.
